title: kernel/mem: Assert ABITS is not below wide_log2

features.name: memory write with insufficient address width
features.description: Code should include a memory write operation where the address width parameter (ABITS) is set to zero or too small for the memory size, causing an out-of-bounds access when checking wide_log2 bits
features.snippet:
memory width 8 size 2 \mem
cell $memwr $auto$:1:$8
  parameter \ABITS 1'0
  parameter \WIDTH 6'010000
  connect \ADDR { }

features.name: memory with wide data relative to address size
features.description: Code should include a memory declaration where the data width (WIDTH) is much larger than what the address width (ABITS) can address, creating a mismatch between memory size and addressable space
features.snippet:
memory width 8 size 2 \mem
cell $memwr
  parameter \ABITS 1'0
  parameter \WIDTH 6'010000

features.name: memory write with multi-bit enable and zero address
features.description: Code should include a memory write cell with a multi-bit enable signal (EN) that is wider than the address port, combined with an empty address connection
